sections.paragraph:
οἴομαι contracted οἶμαι; impf. ᾤμην, ᾤου, ὤετο (all LXX); 1 aor. ᾠήθην ITr 3:3 (Hom.+) to consider someth. to be true but with a component of tentativeness, think, suppose, expect foll. by acc. and inf. (PEleph 13, 6; POxy 1666, 2; Gen 37:7; Job 34:12; Jos., Ant. 1, 323; Just., D. 114, 3; Tat. 16, 1; Ath. 36, 1) J 21:25; 2 Cl 14:2; Dg 3:1. W. inf. foll. (PEleph 12, 1; PFlor 332, 8; POxy 898, 24; 1 Macc 5:61; 2 Macc 7:24; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 117; Just., D. 2, 6; 10, 3; Ath. 36, 1) Phil 1:17; 1 Cl 30:4 (Job 11:2); PtK 2 p. 14, 25; Dg 2:7; 3:4f; 10:3. W. ὅτι foll. (Dio Chrys. 65 [15], 22; Epict. 2, 19, 26; Lucian, Ind. 7 p. 106, Alex. 61 p. 265 al.; Ps.-Aeschines, Ep. 4, 2; Is 57:8; EpArist 227; Tat. 26, 1) Js 1:7; 2 Cl 6:6; 15:1. The passage … εἰς τοῦτο ᾠήθην, ἵνα κτλ. ITr 3:3 is difficult, no doubt because of text damage; in their efforts to make tolerable sense of it, Zahn, Funk, and Bihlmeyer remain closer to the text tradition than does Lghtf. They read οὐκ εἰς τ. ᾠ., ἵνα κτλ. I do not consider myself entitled to, etc.—DELG. M-M s.v. οἶμαι.

impf. impf. = imperfect

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

foll. foll. = followed, following

acc. acc. = accusative

inf. inf. = infinitive

PEleph PEleph = Elephantine-Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

Funk Funk , s. B-D-F—List 6

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 701
